title: SQL injection vulnerability in user lookup
desc: The user lookup query uses string formatting instead of parameterized queries, which could allow SQL injection attacks.

title: Missing password hashing
desc: The password is stored directly without hashing in the register function, violating security best practices.

title: Rate limiter not applied to registration endpoint
desc: The registration endpoint is missing rate limiting, which could allow attackers to create many accounts quickly.

title: Hardcoded JWT secret
desc: The JWT secret is hardcoded in the source code instead of being loaded from environment variables.

suggested_fix.after: JWT_SECRET = os.environ.get("JWT_SECRET") or raise ValueError("JWT_SECRET not set")

title: Missing input validation in login endpoint
desc: The login endpoint does not validate that email and password are provided before processing.

title: Inconsistent error messages
desc: Error messages reveal whether an email exists in the system, which could be used for user enumeration attacks.

title: Session cleanup not implemented
desc: Expired sessions are never cleaned up from the database, which could lead to table bloat over time.

title: Inefficient query in get_user_sessions
desc: The get_user_sessions function performs N+1 queries when loading session details. Should use a JOIN or eager loading.
